mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales signals rising consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate revenues and earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between RSAFS 3-month change and 20-day forward returns is ≤ 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment falling below 5% signals a tight labor market that forces wage inflation, erodes corporate margins, and accelerates the Fed tightening cycle, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day SPX returns occur in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE < 5% and Fed funds rate is unchanged.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and retail sales indicates robust domestic demand that drives economic expansion and corporate earnings, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When HOUST > 1100 and RSAFS > 450000, SPX 20-day returns are not positive in at least 60% of occurrences over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment (CPIAUCSL YoY < 2%), positive payroll growth reflects genuine economic expansion without imminent overheating, boosting equity valuations through higher earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Rank IC of PAYEMS 1-month change vs 20-day forward returns is ≤ 0.04 when CPIAUCSL YoY < 2% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A Federal Funds rate rising above 0.50% signifies a departure from emergency accommodation, increases the discount rate on equity cash flows, and tightens financial conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS > 0.5 and 60-day SPX returns are positive in > 40% of instances over 5 years, or the negative hit rate is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth outpaces industrial production growth, demand outstrips supply capacity, generating inflationary pressures that erode corporate margins and trigger tighter monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth > INDPRO 3-month annualized growth
failure_condition: Rank IC of the demand-supply gap (GDPC1 growth minus INDPRO growth) vs 60-day forward returns is within [-0.02, 0.02] over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal early-cycle residential investment, which has strong multiplier effects on related industries and employment, driving near-term equity market momentum and positive returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC of HOUST 1-month change vs 5-day forward returns is ≤ 0.0 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a low unemployment regime (UNRATE < 5%), any increase in the Fed funds rate from extremely low levels is perceived as premature tightening that threatens growth, reducing equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When UNRATE < 5% and FEDFUNDS rises above 0.25%, SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year evaluation period.
